title: Yacht Rental in Phuket: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Planning a yacht rental in Phuket this year opens up the Andaman Sea from Yacht Haven Marina, with prices for a crewed motor yacht ranging from 35,000 THB for a half-day to over 120,000 THB for a full-day charter. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Phuket or list your own.

How much does it cost to rent a yacht in Phuket?

A crewed 40-ft motor yacht costs around 45,000 THB for a half-day, rising to 90,000 THB for a full-day charter. Budget options include shared tours from 1,500 THB per person, while luxury catamarans can exceed 150,000 THB per day. You can browse boats for your dates to compare verified listings across operators.

  • Shared tour: 1,500, 3,500 THB per person for a half-day trip to Phi Phi or Phang Nga.
  • Small motorboat (half-day, no crew): 8,000, 15,000 THB for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran (full-day, crewed): 60,000, 120,000 THB for 10, 14 guests.
  • Luxury crewed yacht (full-day): 90,000, 200,000 THB for 8, 12 guests.

Where to set off in Phuket?

Phuket has several well-equipped marinas and departure points. Yacht Haven Marina, on the north-east coast near Phang Nga Bay, is a popular base for sailing and motor yachts. Boat Lagoon, also in the east, offers full-service berthing and charter offices. Chalong Pier, on the south-west coast, is the primary hub for speedboat tours to the southern islands. For a short walk-on option, Royal Phuket Marina provides direct access to the open sea and is close to Patong for evening dining. Kata and Rawai beaches are alternative departure points for small craft, though they lack full marina facilities. From any of these, a 30-nautical-mile cruise reaches the Similan Islands, while Koh Mai Thon is just 12 nautical miles south.

Which boat to rent in Phuket?

  1. Speedboat (e.g., Axopar 28 or RiB), ideal for fast island hopping, up to 10 guests, from 12,000 THB half-day. One popular model is the Limpopo, an Axopar 28-footer; check this speedboat for full details.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g., Azimut 40, 60 ft), best for luxury cruising with 8, 12 guests, from 45,000 THB half-day.
  3. Catamaran (e.g., Lagoon 38, 50 ft), stable and spacious for 10, 14 guests, from 60,000 THB full-day.
  4. Traditional longtail, budget-friendly for small groups (up to 6), from 3,000 THB for a local island trip; no booking through boat share platforms.
  5. Sailing yacht (e.g., 38, 50 ft monohull), for a relaxed, wind-powered experience, 6, 8 guests, from 30,000 THB half-day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Phuket?

No, for crewed charters a skipper is included, so no licence is needed; for bareboat (self-drive) rentals you must hold a valid ICC or equivalent certification. The operator confirms exact requirements, but the general principle is that bareboat charters in Thailand require a recognised boating licence or proof of competency.

For bareboat rentals, always present your licence (ICC or Thai equivalent) at the marina; operators may also require a refundable damage deposit of 10,000, 30,000 THB.

When is the best season for yacht rental in Phuket?

The high season runs from November to April, when the north-east monsoon brings calm seas and clear skies. During these months the Andaman Sea is at its best, with average daily temperatures of 28, 32°C and light winds. From May to October the south-west monsoon brings rougher conditions, especially on the west coast, but sheltered areas like Phang Nga Bay remain cruiseable. The Similan Islands are closed for monsoon from mid-October to mid-May for conservation, so plan accordingly.

At a glance

  • Price band: from 1,500 THB (shared) to 200,000+ THB (luxury crewed yacht) per day
  • Peak months: November to April
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hours) to full-day (8 hours) or multi-day charters
  • Capacity: 6, 14 guests typical on private charters
  • Currency: Thai Baht (THB)
  • Distance: 30 nautical miles to Similan Islands from Phuket

Questions about boat rental in Phuket

Can I rent a yacht for a day without a skipper?

Bareboat rentals are available but require a valid ICC or equivalent licence; most visitors opt for a crewed charter that includes a skipper and fuel.

What is included in the rental price?

Crewed charters typically include skipper, fuel, soft drinks, and basic snorkel gear; food and alcohol are extra unless specified in the listing.

Which islands can I visit from Phuket by yacht?

Popular destinations include Phang Nga Bay (James Bond Island), Phi Phi, Koh Mai Thon, and the Similan Islands, each reachable within 1, 2 hours from the main marinas.

Is it safe to rent a boat in Phuket in 2026?

Yes, provided you choose a verified operator with safety equipment, a licensed skipper, and appropriate insurance; BoatHire24 listings include safety checks and reviews.

Whether you are planning a private celebration or a family day out, renting a yacht in Phuket offers access to world-class anchorages and island beauty. To see what is available right now, browse the listings on BoatHire24. If you own a boat in Phuket, consider listing it on the platform to reach travellers from around the world.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Phuket and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
